headline: Rural consumption grew faster than urban consumption from 2011-12 to 2023-24, cutting the urban spending premium from 84% to 70%.
story: MoSPI's HCES 2023-24 shows all-India rural MPCE rising from Rs 1,430 in 2011-12 to Rs 4,122 in 2023-24 (about +188%), while urban MPCE rose from Rs 2,630 to Rs 6,996 (about +166%). The urban-rural difference narrowed from 84% to 70%. This indicates a sizeable catch-up in nominal household consumption outside cities.
surprise: Urban India still spends more per person, but the long gap narrowed because rural spending rose faster over the period.
